topics: Dewan's retirement and the search for a new Dewan
Candidates trying to impress with fake behaviour
The hockey match and the stuck cart
The announcement of the true winner
subject: Hindi

sections.heading: The Quest Begins — Finding a New Dewan
sections.body: In the kingdom of Devgarh, the old and wise Dewan (like a Prime Minister) had served the king for 40 years. He felt old and tired, and worried that if he made mistakes now, his whole life's good name would be ruined. So he requested the king to let him retire. The king agreed but gave him one challenge — YOU must find the next Dewan! Just like in cricket, a great captain must find his own successor.
keyPoints: The old Dewan retired with honour after 40 years of loyal service
The king trusted him so much that he gave him the quest of finding the next Dewan
An advertisement was placed in newspapers across the country inviting candidates

sections.heading: Fake Heroes Enter the Realm
sections.body: Hundreds of people came from all over India — from Punjab, Madras, wearing colourful clothes and fancy hats. Everyone wanted the powerful job! But here is the twist — they all started pretending to be good. One person who used to sleep till 9 AM was now waking up early to watch the sunrise. Someone who hated books was now reading big heavy ones. It was like before a school exam, when students suddenly become very obedient! They were all acting, not being real.
keyPoints: Candidates pretended to be humble, hardworking and well-mannered to impress the judges
They thought — just act good for one month and the job is ours!
But the wise old Dewan was watching everything secretly, like a master wizard spotting fake spells

sections.heading: The Hockey Match — A Dragon Appears
sections.body: One day, the candidates decided to play a hockey match to show off their skills. The match was full of energy — like an IPL match! Players ran fast, blocked hard and sweated a lot. But no one won — the match ended in a draw. After the match, as the players were resting, a poor farmer's cart got badly stuck in a muddy ditch near a stream. This was the real test — the hidden dragon of the quest! Nobody helped the poor farmer. The players saw him but looked away with cold eyes.
keyPoints: The hockey match showed energy but not true character
A poor farmer's cart was stuck in mud — this became the real unexpected test
Most players ignored the farmer — they had no kindness or empathy in their hearts

sections.heading: The True Hero — Janakinath Earns His Power
sections.body: One player had hurt his leg badly during the match and was limping slowly. When HE saw the farmer stuck in the ditch, he didn't walk away. He put down his stick, removed his coat, went into the muddy water even though it came up to his knees and pushed the cart wheel with full strength. The farmer guided the bulls, and together they pulled the cart out! The farmer blessed him and said — if God wills, the Dewan's job will be yours! This one act of courage and kindness showed his true character — just like in cricket, one match-winning over reveals a true champion.
keyPoints: Janakinath helped even though he was injured — he showed real bravery and compassion
He got deep into the mud without caring about his clothes or pain
The farmer's blessing was like a magical spell — it turned out to be true!

sections.heading: The Winner Is Announced — Mastery Rewarded
sections.body: When the month ended, the king held a grand royal court. All the candidates came dressed in their best clothes, hearts beating fast like before a big exam result. Sardar Sujansingh stood up and announced — the new Dewan is Janakinath! He explained that a true leader needs two things — a kind heart AND inner strength. Janakinath showed both when he helped a poor farmer while being injured. The officials looked at Janakinath with respect; the other candidates looked with jealousy. The quest was complete — the true hero had been found!
keyPoints: The king's announcement revealed that the real test was never the hockey match — it was kindness in an unexpected moment
A good leader needs both compassion for the poor AND courage to face difficulties
Premchand wrote this story to teach us that true character shows in small everyday actions, not in fake performances

summary: This wonderful story by Premchand is called 'Pariksha' (The Test). The kingdom of Devgarh needed a new Dewan. Hundreds of people came and started pretending to be good — waking up early, reading books, being polite — all just to get the job. But the wise old Dewan was watching quietly. The real test came unexpectedly during a hockey match, when a poor farmer's cart got stuck in the mud. Everyone ignored him, but one young man named Janakinath — even though he was limping from a leg injury — jumped into the muddy water and pushed the cart out. This one act of true kindness and bravery earned him the Dewan's post. The story teaches us that real power and mastery come from a good heart and inner strength, not from pretending or showing off. Like a true hero on a quest, Janakinath did not seek glory — he just did the right thing!
